mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in nonfarm payrolls signals rising household income and near-term earnings support, causing higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when payroll growth remains above zero.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot 145554.0 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS changes are followed by lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative PAYEMS changes, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment signals deterioration in labor demand and expected consumption, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days after labor-market weakness becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot 4.8 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot 0.65 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS upward crossings are followed by higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-crossing observations, or if the sign of the estimated coefficient is positive in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate stronger household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when sales momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot 472142.0 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth crossings above 1.0% do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than other months, or if hit rate is below 50% over a 5-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals improving goods-sector output and operating leverage, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when production growth is broad enough to persist.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot 104.5548 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 6-month growth crossings above 0% are followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal underperforms unconditional 60-day returns in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the federal funds rate also rises, inflation pressure and policy tightening jointly compress equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month growth exceeds 1.5% and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot 244.158 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot 0.65
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase state has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if an interaction term between the two proxies is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after housing momentum breaks lower.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot 1226.0 as of two months before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST 3-month growth crossings below -5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or if average triggered returns exceed baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP is expanding and unemployment is falling, broad demand growth and labor strength reinforce earnings expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth is positive and UNRATE falls over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot 16804.8 as of four months before decision date and UNRATE latest snapshot 4.8
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of positive GDPC1 growth and falling UNRATE fails to produce higher average 5-day S&P 500 returns than either condition alone, or if the interaction coefficient is nonpositive in rolling tests.
